local_names.romanized_local: Yànmài Zhōu (Chinese); Bubur Oat (Malay); Ōṭs kanji (Tamil); Oats Daliya (Hindi)

cuisine: Western / International (widely consumed in Singapore as a breakfast staple)
food_subgroup: Whole grain oats — rolled oats (oat groats steamed and flattened) cooked as plain water-based porridge; beta-glucan rich

ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for plain rolled oats porridge; FoodOn and FoodEx2 anchors are sufficient.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard bowl of plain rolled oats porridge prepared from 40g dry rolled oats cooked with 210ml water, yielding approximately 250g cooked porridge.

nutrient_source.title: Cereals, oats, regular and quick, unenriched, cooked with water (includes boiling and microwaving), without salt — USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 173904
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, SR Legacy Foods Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy FDC 173904 reports cooked oatmeal (water, no salt, no milk) per 100g. Values used directly. This matches the preparation described: 40g dry rolled oats + 210ml water, cooked 3–5 minutes, plain.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Rolled Oats (cooked with water) — University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: University of Sydney GI Database reports rolled oats (cooked with water) GI = 55 (range 51–58). GL per 250g serving calculated as GI (55) × available carbohydrate per serving (24g) / 100 = 13.2. Beta-glucan content approximately 1.5g per 250g bowl.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (2mg/100g; ~5mg per 250g bowl, plain, no salt added) and beta-glucan with mild ACE-inhibitory peptide precursor activity
hypertension.guidance: Plain rolled oats porridge is an excellent breakfast choice for hypertensive patients: sodium is negligible (~5mg per bowl), magnesium (~50mg per bowl) supports vascular tone, and beta-glucan (~1.5g per bowl) provides mild blood pressure benefit — avoid adding salt or high-sodium toppings.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: GI ~55 (low), GL ~13.2 per bowl; beta-glucan 1.5g per bowl is below the ≥3g/day therapeutic threshold for optimal glycaemic benefit
type2_diabetes.guidance: Rolled oats porridge (GI ~55, GL ~13.2) is suitable for Type 2 Diabetes management as a low-GI breakfast; however, beta-glucan content at 1.5g per bowl is below the ≥3g/day threshold shown to significantly blunt postprandial glucose — two bowls or supplementation with oat bran would be needed for full therapeutic effect; prefer rolled or steel-cut oats (GI ~42) over instant oats (GI ~83).

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Beta-glucan soluble fibre (1.5g per bowl), low saturated fat (0.1g/100g), zero cholesterol, and low sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Plain rolled oats porridge is cardioprotective: saturated fat is negligible (0.25g per bowl), cholesterol is zero, and beta-glucan at 1.5g per bowl contributes to LDL-cholesterol reduction (FDA-approved health claim requires ≥3g/day beta-glucan; two bowls per day achieves this threshold) — recommended as a daily breakfast for CVD risk reduction.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Beta-glucan prebiotic activity supporting gut microbiome (RA-gut axis); avenanthramides with mild anti-inflammatory properties
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Rolled oats porridge is suitable for rheumatoid arthritis patients: avenanthramides provide mild anti-inflammatory activity, beta-glucan supports gut microbiome health relevant to the RA-gut axis, and low sodium and saturated fat reduce systemic inflammatory load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Rolled oats porridge (plain, water-cooked) is a whole grain breakfast dish prepared by simmering 40g of rolled oats (oat groats that have been steamed and flattened) in 210ml of water for 3–5 minutes, yielding approximately 250g of cooked porridge. The texture is soft, creamy, and slightly chewy with a mild, neutral oat flavour. Per 250g bowl: approximately 150 kcal, 5g protein, 2g fat, 27g carbohydrate, 3g dietary fibre (including ~1.5g beta-glucan), and only ~5mg sodium. Glycemic index ~55 (low; University of Sydney GI Database), glycemic load ~13.2 per bowl. Beta-glucan content of 1.5g per bowl is below the ≥3g/day therapeutic threshold for LDL reduction and optimal glycaemic benefit — two bowls per day or addition of oat bran achieves the clinical dose. Rolled oats (GI ~55) are significantly better than instant oats (GI ~83 ≈ white rice) for blood glucose management; steel-cut oats (GI ~42) are superior but less convenient. Widely consumed in Singapore as a breakfast staple, available at NTUC FairPrice, Cold Storage, and hotel breakfast buffets (Quaker, Bob's Red Mill, Uncle Tobys brands). Suitable for vegetarian, vegan, halal, and dairy-free diets. Contains gluten (oats). Source: USDA SR Legacy FDC 173904. Confidence: HIGH.
common_user_queries: rolled oats vs steel cut oats GI

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from USDA SR Legacy FDC 173904 (cooked oatmeal, water, no salt), which is an exact match for the preparation described. GI value of 55 is from the University of Sydney GI Database for rolled oats cooked with water. All core macros, secondary nutrients, and micronutrients are fully populated from the approved primary source.
notes: Per 100g values represent cooked porridge (40g dry rolled oats + 210ml water = ~250g cooked). Sugar_g is confirmed zero per USDA FDC 173904 — plain oats cooked in water contain no added sugar and negligible free sugars. Cholesterol_mg is naturally zero as this is a plant-based whole grain food. Vitamin_b12_mcg is naturally zero as oats are a plant food containing no B12. Vitamin_c_mg and vitamin_a_mcg_rae are confirmed zero per USDA FDC 173904 for plain cooked oats. Vitamin_d_mcg is naturally zero in unfortified plain oats. Trans_fat_g is confirmed zero per USDA FDC 173904. Monounsaturated_fat_g (~0.3g/100g) and polyunsaturated_fat_g (~0.3g/100g) estimated from USDA FDC 173904 fatty acid profile. Water_g (~84g/100g) estimated from USDA FDC 173904 moisture data for cooked oatmeal. Beta-glucan content estimated at ~0.6g/100g cooked (1.5g per 250g bowl), derived from published oat beta-glucan content of ~3.7g/100g dry oats × 40g/250g dilution factor. GL per serving calculated as GI (55) × available carbohydrate per serving (24g) / 100 = 13.2. FoodEx2 code A0OAP retained from input; defensible as closest available code for cooked rolled oats porridge. SNOMED CT code not identified; FoodOn and FoodEx2 anchors are sufficient. This record is classified as Western/International cuisine consumed in Singapore context; multilingual names provided for English, Chinese (Simplified and Traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi per Singaporean dish requirements.
